title: juncus tenuis subsp. dichotomus
intro: Juncus tenuis subsp. dichotomus, also known as jointed rush or forked rush, is a subspecies of the Juncus tenuis plant, commonly found in wetland areas and characterized by its jointed stems and forked branches. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Juncus tenuis subsp. dichotomus include its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve congestion and soothe irritated airways. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support lung health and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
